trace.sql:
SELECT SUM(oi.net_amount) AS total_revenue
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: What is our revenue broken down by customer region?

trace.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY orders DESC

SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orders
FROM orders
WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY orders DESC

SELECT c.region, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  AND o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY c.region
ORDER BY revenue DESC

records.question: How much revenue came from orders shipped by FedEx?

trace.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orders, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL) AS deleted FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY status

SELECT SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM shipments s
    WHERE s.order_id = o.order_id
      AND s.carrier = 'FedEx'
  );

WITH item_totals AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(net_amount) AS order_revenue
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
), fedex_orders AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT order_id
  FROM shipments
  WHERE carrier = 'FedEx'
)
SELECT SUM(it.order_revenue) AS revenue
FROM fedex_orders f
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = f.order_id
JOIN item_totals it ON it.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: What is the total quantity of items sold, grouped by shipping carrier?

trace.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL) AS deleted_n FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY status
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shipment_rows, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS shipped_orders, COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS extra_shipments FROM shipments

SELECT s.carrier, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM shipments s JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = s.order_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL AND lower(o.status) NOT IN ('cancelled','canceled') GROUP BY s.carrier ORDER BY s.carrier
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM orders WHERE deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY status ORDER BY status

WITH shipped_orders AS (SELECT DISTINCT order_id FROM shipments) SELECT o.status, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders FROM orders o JOIN shipped_orders so ON so.order_id=o.order_id WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY o.status ORDER BY o.status

WITH order_qty AS (SELECT order_id, SUM(quantity) AS qty FROM order_items GROUP BY order_id), order_carrier AS (SELECT DISTINCT order_id, carrier FROM shipments) SELECT oc.carrier, SUM(oq.qty) AS total_quantity FROM order_carrier oc JOIN orders o ON o.order_id=oc.order_id JOIN order_qty oq ON oq.order_id=oc.order_id WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL AND lower(o.status) NOT IN ('cancelled','canceled') GROUP BY oc.carrier ORDER BY oc.carrier

WITH order_carrier AS (SELECT order_id, COUNT(DISTINCT carrier) AS carriers FROM shipments GROUP BY order_id) SELECT carriers, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM order_carrier GROUP BY carriers ORDER BY carriers

WITH order_qty AS (SELECT order_id, SUM(quantity) AS qty FROM order_items GROUP BY order_id), order_carrier AS (SELECT DISTINCT order_id, carrier FROM shipments) SELECT oc.carrier, SUM(oq.qty) AS total_quantity FROM order_carrier oc JOIN orders o ON o.order_id=oc.order_id JOIN order_qty oq ON oq.order_id=oc.order_id WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL AND lower(o.status) = 'completed' GROUP BY oc.carrier ORDER BY oc.carrier

records.question: Which three regions generated the most revenue?

trace.sql:
SELECT c.region, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY c.region
ORDER BY revenue DESC
LIMIT 3;

records.question: How many orders have we received?

trace.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) AS non_deleted_orders, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL) AS deleted_orders FROM orders;

records.question: What is our average revenue per order?

trace.sql:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orders, SUM(CASE WHEN deleted_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS not_deleted
FROM orders
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY status;

SELECT 
  COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS completed_orders,
  SUM(oi.net_amount) AS total_revenue,
  SUM(oi.net_amount) / COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS avg_revenue_per_order
FROM orders o
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  AND o.status = 'completed';

records.question: What is our total revenue from completed orders?

trace.sql:
SELECT SUM(oi.net_amount) AS total_revenue
FROM orders o
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE LOWER(o.status) = 'completed'
  AND o.deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: How many items were shipped via UPS?

trace.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS line_items, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.item_id) AS distinct_items, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders
FROM shipments s
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = s.order_id
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE s.carrier = 'UPS';

WITH ups_orders AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT order_id
  FROM shipments
  WHERE carrier = 'UPS'
), item_counts AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(quantity) AS item_quantity
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
)
SELECT SUM(ic.item_quantity) AS items_shipped_via_ups
FROM ups_orders u
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = u.order_id
JOIN item_counts ic ON ic.order_id = u.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: Which shipping carrier handled the most orders?

trace.sql:
SELECT carrier, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS orders_handled
FROM shipments
GROUP BY carrier
ORDER BY orders_handled DESC, carrier
LIMIT 1;

records.question: What is our revenue per region, for completed orders only?

trace.sql:
SELECT c.region, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE lower(o.status) = 'completed'
  AND o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY c.region
ORDER BY c.region;

records.question: How many distinct customers have placed an order?

trace.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS distinct_customers_with_orders FROM orders;
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS distinct_customers_with_orders FROM orders WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: What is our revenue per shipping carrier?

trace.sql:
SELECT s.carrier, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM shipments s
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = s.order_id
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY s.carrier
ORDER BY revenue DESC;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shipments, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS shipped_orders, COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS extra_shipments FROM shipments;
SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS shipment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT carrier) AS carriers FROM shipments GROUP BY order_id HAVING COUNT(*)>1 OR COUNT(DISTINCT carrier)>1 LIMIT 10;

WITH order_revenue AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(net_amount) AS order_revenue
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
)
SELECT s.carrier, SUM(orv.order_revenue) AS revenue
FROM shipments s
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = s.order_id
JOIN order_revenue orv ON orv.order_id = s.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY s.carrier
ORDER BY revenue DESC;

WITH order_revenue AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(net_amount) AS order_revenue
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
), shipment_counts AS (
  SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS shipment_count
  FROM shipments
  GROUP BY order_id
)
SELECT s.carrier, SUM(orv.order_revenue / sc.shipment_count) AS allocated_revenue
FROM shipments s
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = s.order_id
JOIN order_revenue orv ON orv.order_id = s.order_id
JOIN shipment_counts sc ON sc.order_id = s.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY s.carrier
ORDER BY allocated_revenue DESC;

WITH order_revenue AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(net_amount) AS order_revenue
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
), order_carriers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT order_id, carrier
  FROM shipments
), carrier_counts AS (
  SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS carrier_count
  FROM order_carriers
  GROUP BY order_id
)
SELECT oc.carrier, SUM(orv.order_revenue / cc.carrier_count) AS allocated_revenue
FROM order_carriers oc
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oc.order_id
JOIN order_revenue orv ON orv.order_id = oc.order_id
JOIN carrier_counts cc ON cc.order_id = oc.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY oc.carrier
ORDER BY allocated_revenue DESC;

WITH order_revenue AS (
  SELECT oi.order_id, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS order_revenue
  FROM order_items oi
  JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
  WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  GROUP BY oi.order_id
)
SELECT SUM(order_revenue) AS total_revenue, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM order_revenue;

records.question: How much revenue has each product generated?

trace.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orders, SUM(CASE WHEN deleted_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS not_deleted FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY status

SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.deleted_at IS NULL
GROUP BY oi.product_id
ORDER BY oi.product_id

SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.deleted_at IS NULL AND oi.product_id BETWEEN 51 AND 100
GROUP BY oi.product_id ORDER BY oi.product_id

SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.deleted_at IS NULL AND oi.product_id BETWEEN 101 AND 150
GROUP BY oi.product_id ORDER BY oi.product_id

SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(oi.net_amount) AS revenue
FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.deleted_at IS NULL AND oi.product_id BETWEEN 151 AND 200
GROUP BY oi.product_id ORDER BY oi.product_id

records.question: What is our total revenue, counting only orders that were actually shipped?

trace.sql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(oi.net_amount), 0) AS total_revenue
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM shipments s
    WHERE s.order_id = o.order_id
  );

WITH order_revenue AS (
  SELECT order_id, SUM(net_amount) AS revenue
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY order_id
), shipped_orders AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT order_id
  FROM shipments
)
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(orv.revenue), 0) AS total_revenue
FROM order_revenue orv
JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = orv.order_id
JOIN shipped_orders so ON so.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL;

records.question: What is the average number of shipments per order?

trace.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM shipments) AS total_shipments, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM shipments)::DOUBLE / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS avg_shipments_per_order FROM orders;

SELECT COUNT(s.shipment_id)::DOUBLE / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id), 0) AS avg_shipments_per_order
FROM orders o
LEFT JOIN shipments s ON s.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL;
